title: diphasiastrum complanatum subsp. issleri
intro: Diphasiastrum complanatum subsp. issleri, also known as Issler's forked lepidodendron, is a subspecies of a type of fern-like vascular plant, belonging to the family Lycopodiaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to its rich composition of organic compounds and essential oils. The health benefits of Diphasiastrum complanatum subsp. issleri include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and reduce fever. In herbal preparations, this plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
